text: Dear Andrew, My sincere gratitude for you. Couple of months back I purchased the PMP CRAM course+ Simulator package. Followed your study plan. text: Passed my exam yesterday with AT in each section, only took me 2 hours and 15 minutes and I took my time and went back to review 5-10 questions in each section. As long as it takes to read the question and available answers should be all the time you need

text:
Hi Andrew
I need a clarification for a question, can u pls tell which ans is correct

A project has been initiated, and the project team is ready to get to work, but all their vendor contracts are stuck in procurement. Which two actions should the project manager take? (Choose 2)
Add a risk to the risk log.
A project has been initiated, and the project team is ready to get to work, but all their vendor contracts are stuck in procurement. Which two actions should the project manager take? (Choose 2)
A. Add a risk to the risk log.
B. Escalate the authorization of the contracts to the project sponsor.
C. Note the delay as an issue in the issue log.
D. create contracts to be authorized by the project.
E. Pay vendors from the project contingency budget.

text: Why not work on QUALITY questions, not a quantity of questions? 120 solid questions to show what one can do, and add experience as part of the certification. Instead of becoming progressive and fair, PMI has opted to be rigid and unfairly selective.

text:
Okay well I have a question!!!!️ 
I’m currently studying for my CAPM exam, started beginning of last month and already 60-70% done with Andrew’s study course on UDEMY.
I plan on taking his exam prep on TIA after the fact and shoot for my exam date in Late Jan-Feb.

I feel like there’s so much great resources out there to utilize such as Andrew’s own courses on PMP prep and if u wait after July it’ll be more difficult since there won’t be as much resources out there to study from !!!!

Lmk guys
